curation_notes: Primary location derived from the patch diff which modifies the _attachment_file_name property in the _AttachmentPartitioner class in unstructured/partition/msg.py. The pre-patch code returns the raw attachment filename without sanitization, and this filename is used in os.path.join to construct a file path for writing attachment contents to disk. A sibling file (unstructured/partition/email.py) has a similar _attachment_file_name property but does not write attachments to disk via os.path.join — it uses in-memory BytesIO instead — so it is not vulnerable to the same path traversal attack. The vulnerability class path-traversal matches the CWE-22 and CWE-73 classifications and is confirmed by the patch which adds os.path.basename sanitization.
